decisions.decision: Define the new backend-wide EventTrade exposure cap in `backend/server.py` as `max_position_size = 100.0`, colocated with the existing module-level trading/risk constants so later validation and serialization can share one default source of truth.
alternatives_rejected: Leave the constant unspecified until the write-path work lands.
Choose a different default that is disconnected from the existing `ACCOUNT_RISK_LIMIT = 100.0` scale.
Hide the value inside route-local logic instead of a reusable global constant.

decisions.decision: Add a dedicated in-memory exposure projection, separate from `ACCOUNT_RISK`, with account and position builder helpers that only materialize live EventTrade state and are cleared by `reset_state()`.
alternatives_rejected: Extend `ACCOUNT_RISK` to also store EventTrade positions.
Recompute exposure from historical orders on every read.
Keep exposure state outside the normal in-memory reset lifecycle.

decisions.decision: Model each `(accountId, marketId, outcomeId)` exposure slice as one signed net position where buys add size, sells subtract size, exact-zero net positions are deleted immediately, and accounts with no remaining live positions are pruned from the exposure store so `/exposure` can later return `404 account_not_found`.
alternatives_rejected: Retain zero-size positions as historical placeholders.
Keep empty exposure accounts and return `200` with an empty document.
Track separate long and short buckets instead of one signed net position.

decisions.decision: Serialize exposure through additive helpers that return `{account, meta}` with account-level `maxPositionSize` and `updatedAt`, plus a flat `positions` list sorted lexically by `(marketId, outcomeId)` and populated from the projection with `netSize`, `absSize`, `lastTradePrice`, `updatedAt`, `lastOrderId`, and `lastCommandId`.
alternatives_rejected: Return only aggregate account fields without per-outcome rows.
Expose an empty envelope for missing exposure accounts.

decisions.decision: Add market-scoped exposure cleanup helpers that mirror the existing resolution-settlement seam by removing all live positions for a resolved market, updating account timestamps as needed, and pruning now-empty exposure accounts without touching historical `ORDERS` or `EVENTS`.
alternatives_rejected: Leave resolved-market positions in the live exposure projection.
Inline resolution cleanup inside the resolve command instead of reusable helpers.
Delete historical trade/order records when cleaning live exposure.

steps.task: Add the new global `max_position_size` constant and a dedicated top-level exposure store in `backend/server.py`, wired into `reset_state()` beside the existing in-memory read models.

Implement account/position builder and ensure helpers for the exposure projection, following the existing `build_account_risk_state(...)` and `ensure_account_risk_state(...)` conventions but scoped to live EventTrade positions only.

Add signed-size math helpers for EventTrade positions, including net-size calculation, exact-zero row deletion, account pruning when the final live position disappears, and field updates for `lastTradePrice`, `updatedAt`, `lastOrderId`, and `lastCommandId`.

Implement exposure serializers that transform the internal projection into the planned `{account, meta}` response shape with deterministic lexical `(marketId, outcomeId)` ordering and account-level metadata derived from the new constant and projection timestamps.

Add reusable market-resolution cleanup helpers that remove all exposure positions for one market and prune empty accounts, so later resolve-path and route work can call a single backend primitive instead of duplicating cleanup logic.
